title: King Charles III Will Be Safe During US Visit, Says President Trump
summary: President Donald Trump says King Charles III will be very safe when he visits the United States. The King and Queen are coming for a four-day state visit. They will start in Washington, D.C., at the White House. President Trump and his wife Melania will greet them. The visit happens after a scary event. A man with a gun got into a place where President Trump was. But no one was badly hurt. The President says the White House is a very safe place. The King and Queen called the Trumps to say they were sorry about the scary event. The visit is important for the United Kingdom and the United States. It will help them be friends again. The King will also go to New York to visit the 9/11 Memorial. There are many British and American flags around the White House. The visit will show that the two countries are good partners.

keywords.explanation: A special trip by a king or queen to another country to meet its leaders.
The home and office of the President of the United States in Washington, D.C.
A place in New York that remembers the people who died in the attacks on September 11, 2001.
Two countries that work together and help each other.

questions.question: Where will the King and Queen first go in the US?

options: To help the UK and US be friends

questions.question: What did President Trump say about the White House?
How long is the state visit?

background_read: King Charles III is the king of the United Kingdom. The United Kingdom is a country in Europe. The United States is another country. Sometimes the leaders of these countries visit each other. A state visit is a special trip. The king meets the president. They talk about how to be good friends. The White House is where the US president lives and works. It is in Washington, D.C. The 9/11 Memorial remembers a sad day in 2001 when planes hit buildings.
Article_Structure: WHO: King Charles III and Queen Camilla are visiting the United States. President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will welcome them.
WHAT: A four-day state visit to strengthen friendship between the UK and US.
WHERE: The visit begins at the White House in Washington, D.C., and includes a trip to the 9/11 Memorial in New York.
WHY: To help the two countries be good partners after some difficult times.

perspectives.description: President Trump says the King will be safe and the visit is great.
Sir Christian Turner says the visit will show shared history and make both countries safer and happier.
